# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - develop
      - feature/*

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm' # Caches npm dependencies

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Use npm ci for clean installs in CI

      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Build application
        run: npm run build

      - name: Upload build artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: application-build
          path: build/ # Adjust this path to your actual build output directory

  deploy-staging:
    needs: build-and-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging # Designates a GitHub environment for staging

    steps:
      - name: Download build artifact
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: application-build
          path: ./app-build/ # Downloads the artifact to a local directory

      - name: Deploy to Staging
        run: echo "Deploying build artifact from ./app-build/ to staging environment..."
        # Replace the above echo command with your actual deployment logic.
        # This could involve:
        # - scp to a server
        # - aws s3 sync
        # - docker push and kubernetes deploy
        # - specific cloud provider CLI commands (e.g., gcloud, az cli)
        # For example, if deploying to an EC2 instance via SSH:
        # - uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master
        #   with:
        #     host: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SSH_HOST }}
        #     username: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SSH_USER }}
        #     key: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SSH_KEY }}
        #     script: |
        #       rm -rf /var/www/html/*
        #       mv ./app-build/* /var/www/html/
        #       systemctl restart your-app-service
